records.ety: [OF. surveoir, surveer; sur, sor, over, E. sur + veoir, veeir, to see, F. voir, L. videre. See Sur-, and Vision, and cf. Supervise.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Surveyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Surveying.]

defs: To inspect, or take a view of; to view with attention, as from a high place; to overlook; as, to stand on a hill, and survey the surrounding country.
quotes: Round he surveys and well might, where he stood, So high above.

defs: To view with a scrutinizing eye; to examine.
quotes: With such altered looks, . . . All pale and speechless, he surveyed me round.

defs: To examine with reference to condition, situation, value, etc.; to examine and ascertain the state of; as, to survey a building in order to determine its value and exposure to loss by fire.

To determine the form, extent, position, etc., of, as a tract of land, a coast, harbor, or the like, by means of linear and angular measurements, and the application of the principles of geometry and trigonometry; as, to survey land or a coast.
To examine and ascertain, as the boundaries and royalties of a manor, the tenure of the tenants, and the rent and value of the same.

records.ety: [Formerly accentuated universally on the last syllable, and still so accented by many speakers.]

defs: The act of surveying; a general view, as from above.
quotes: Under his proud survey the city lies.

defs: A particular view; an examination, especially an official examination, of all the parts or particulars of a thing, with a design to ascertain the condition, quantity, or quality; as, a survey of the stores of a ship; a survey of roads and bridges; a survey of buildings.

The operation of finding the contour, dimensions, position, or other particulars of, as any part of the earth's surface, whether land or water; also, a measured plan and description of any portion of country, or of a road or line through it.
